content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Apr 1, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on April 1, 2026, focused on expanding the ElizaOS ecosystem through new plugin proposals for AI marketplaces and trust-verification layers. Community contributors are actively proposing integrations for GPU rental, token swapping, and cryptographic identity to enhance agent autonomy and security.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS
*No code changes, features, or refactoring were merged into the repository on this date.*

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*None.*

## CLOSED ISSUES
*None.*

## NEW ISSUES
**AI Marketplace Integration**
*   [#6700](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6700): A proposal for a `plugin-maxia` to enable native access to the MAXIA AI-to-AI marketplace, supporting token swaps across 7 chains, GPU rental, and AI service trading.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
**Agent Trust and Identity Frameworks**
*   [#6688](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6688): Ongoing discussion regarding the integration of AgentID for cryptographic identity. Recent updates clarify that trust levels are dynamic and capability-based rather than static. Contributors are debating the alignment of behavioral fingerprinting metrics, specifically comparing AgentID’s L0-L4 tiers with the SINT Protocol’s CSML (Composite Safety-Model Latency) scoring system.
*   [#6501](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/6501): Discussion continues on the MoltBridge integration. The contributor reported that MoltBridge is now listed in the official A2A registry and highlighted that other frameworks (CrewAI) have adopted similar trust-scoring models, reinforcing the need for a native ElizaOS plugin for trust-verified agent discovery.
